channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Delhi Judge Aman Kumar Sharma Dies by Suicide in Safdarjung Area
item.description: In a sad incident, Judge Aman Kumar Sharma of Delhi was found hanging in his residence in the Safdarjung area on Saturday. The police have confirmed that it is a case of suicide. According to the info...

item.title: Kentucky Derby expands premium offerings as luxury suites sell out
item.description: This year’s Kentucky Derby, marking its 152nd running, is experiencing a significant focus on luxury experiences as part of a nearly $1 billion investment plan aimed at reinvigorating interest in hors...

item.title: West Bengal Tea Garden Workers Seek ILO Intervention Over Non-Payment of Wages and Benefits
item.description: In West Bengal, the Paschim Banga Cha Majoor Samity (PBCMS), a non-political trade union representing tea plantation workers, has submitted a formal representation to the International Labour Organiza...

item.title: Kapamilya Stars Share Their Personal Fears and Challenges
item.description: Several Kapamilya stars recently opened up about their personal fears and anxieties, shedding light on the vulnerabilities that they usually keep private. The article features insights from well-known...

item.title: Canada to compete for bronze after semifinal loss at World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship
item.description: Canada’s bid for its first World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship gold medal ended on Friday after a 6-4 loss to Sweden in the semifinals. Representing Gimli, Manitoba, Kadriana and Colton Lott fell...

item.title: Calls for Mediation Intensify in Dispute Over Edgar Lungu’s Burial with Mudolo Proposed as Mediator
item.description: The protracted dispute over the final resting place of former Zambian president Edgar Lungu has intensified, with Willah Mudolo emerging as a potential mediator. Mudolo, who assumed leadership of Zamb...

item.title: Sheffield Wednesday completes takeover, ending Chansiri's troubled tenure
item.description: Sheffield Wednesday have exited administration following the completion of a takeover by a US-based consortium led by David Storch. This marks the end of Dejphon Chansiri’s controversial ownership, wh...

item.title: President Samia to Address Workers Amid Wage and Economic Concerns
item.description: Today, President Samia Suluhu Hassan dey lead Labour Day celebrations for Njombe, and workers dem dey expect clear signals about wages and economic matters. Since she take office in March 2021, she do...

item.title: The 1826 Red River flood and its lasting impact on early Manitoba settlement
item.description: The article recounts the devastating Red River flood of 1826, considered the largest in the watershed’s recorded history and described by historians as a once-in-a-millennium event. Triggered by an un...

item.title: Growth in Kenya’s Mobile Money Use Driven by Rising Peer-to-Peer Transactions
item.description: Kenya’s mobile money sector continued to expand strongly in 2025, with subscriptions reaching 51.4 million, according to the Kenya National Bureau of Statistics Economic Survey 2026. This marks a 21.4...

item.title: Dodoma Region's Growing Economic Opportunities Highlighted by Finance Minister
item.description: The Minister for Finance, Mr. Khamis Mussa Omar, recently visited Dodoma's Regional Commissioner, Rosemary Senyamule, where they discussed the region's immense economic and social potential. Omar emph...

item.title: Four people hospitalised following early morning shooting in Brixton
item.description: In the early hours of Saturday morning, four individuals were injured in a drive-by shooting on Coldharbour Lane in Brixton, south London. The victims, aged 21, 25, 47, and 70, were taken to hospital,...

item.title: Calls for investigation into alleged deaths at Nigerian army-run camp amid claims of poor conditions
item.description: Amnesty International has called on Nigerian authorities to investigate claims that at least 150 people, mostly children, have died in an army-run camp in Yikpata, Kwara state. The camp reportedly hou...

item.title: Ipswich Town Secure Automatic Promotion to Premier League After Final Day Win
item.description: Ipswich Town don finally secure their return to the Premier League after them destroy Queens Park Rangers 3-0 for the last day of Championship season on Saturday. This win mean say Ipswich go enter En...

item.title: Ross Davidson, ex-Spandau Ballet singer, sentenced to 14 years for sexual crimes
item.description: Ross Davidson, formerly a member of the 1980s band Spandau Ballet, has been sentenced to 14 years in prison following convictions for rape and sexual assault. The court proceedings revealed a series o...

item.title: Man charged over Dunmurry police station bomb appears in court
item.description: A 66-year-old man, Kieran Smyth from Beechmount Avenue in Belfast, has appeared before Lisburn Magistrates’ Court in connection with a car bomb attack outside Dunmurry Police Station. He faces multipl...

item.title: Rivers NLC calls for end of contributory pension scheme over transparency issues
item.description: Alex Agwanwor, wey be chairman of Nigeria Labour Congress (NLC) for Rivers State, don call make dem terminate the contributory pension scheme wey dey run for the state. He talk this one during the 202...

item.title: Illegal Traveller Site Established on Protected Wildlife Field During Bank Holiday
item.description: During the recent three-day Bank Holiday weekend, a four-acre field near Felsted in Essex, noted for its wildlife including protected great-crested newts and rare albino fallow deer, was rapidly trans...

item.title: Carney explains shift in stance on Iran conflict as Trump’s objectives evolved
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney recently addressed his evolving stance on the U.S. war in Iran, explaining that his support for the conflict changed as the objectives of U.S. President Donald Trump became ...

item.title: Egbin Power Plant Suspended Operations After Fatal Accident In Pump House Lagoon
item.description: Egbin Power Plant, wey be Nigeria’s biggest thermal electricity station, don stop operations since Tuesday, April 28, 2026, after one contractor die for inside the plant’s lagoon-based pump house. The...
